message:
test(lifecycle): health/diag agree on DB + index presence (ibuuh.23)

Another cross-surface row. cass ships two diagnostic JSON surfaces
that both report whether the on-disk DB and lexical index exist —
cass health --json at .db.exists and .state.index.exists, cass diag
--json at .database.exists and .index.exists. When these disagree one
of them is reading stale or cached state, which leaves operators
unable to tell which diagnostic to trust. Against an isolated empty
HOME both surfaces MUST report false on both fields.

New test health_and_diag_agree_on_db_and_index_presence cross-checks
.db.exists, .index.exists across the two surfaces and also asserts
the empty-HOME shape (both false) so the test doesn't become a
tautological no-op if the extraction paths break.

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix: 59/59 pass, 2 stable re-runs.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.23; bead stays
open for scheduler-backed rows still blocked on ibuuh.30 / ibuuh.32.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
